authors_text: Arjun Nagendran,Scott Compton,William Follette,Artem Golenchenko,Anna Compton,Jonathan Grizou
title: Metaversal Learning Environments: Measuring, predicting and improving interpersonal effectiveness
title_zh: Metaversal 学习环境：测量、预测和提高人际效能
abstract: Experiential learning has been known to be an engaging and effective modality for personal and professional development. The Metaverse provides ample opportunities for the creation of environments in which such experiential learning can occur. In this work, we introduce a novel architecture that combines Artificial intelligence and Virtual Reality to create a highly immersive and efficient learning experience using avatars. The framework allows us to measure the interpersonal effectiveness of an individual interacting with the avatar. We first present a small pilot study and its results which were used to enhance the framework. We then present a larger study using the enhanced framework to measure, assess, and predict the interpersonal effectiveness of individuals interacting with an avatar. Results reveal that individuals with deficits in their interpersonal effectiveness show a significant improvement in performance after multiple interactions with an avatar. The results also reveal that individuals interact naturally with avatars within this framework, and exhibit similar behavioral traits as they would in the real world. We use this as a basis to analyze the underlying audio and video data streams of individuals during these interactions. Finally, we extract relevant features from these data and present a machine-learning based approach to predict interpersonal effectiveness during human-avatar conversation. We conclude by discussing the implications of these findings to build beneficial applications for the real world.

We conclude by discussing the implications of these findings to build beneficial applications for the real world.
abstract_zh: 众所周知，体验式学习是个人和职业发展的一种有吸引力且有效的方式。元宇宙为创建可以发生这种体验式学习的环境提供了充足的机会。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种新颖的架构，该架构结合了人工智能和虚拟现实，利用化身创建高度沉浸式且高效的学习体验。该框架使我们能够衡量个人与化身互动的人际有效性。我们首先提出一项小型试点研究及其用于增强框架的结果。然后，我们提出了一项更大规模的研究，使用增强的框架来衡量、评估和预测个体与化身互动的人际有效性。结果表明，人际交往效率存在缺陷的个体在与虚拟形象进行多次互动后表现出显着改善。结果还表明，个体在这个框架内与化身自然地互动，并表现出与现实世界相似的行为特征。我们以此为基础来分析个体在这些交互过程中的底层音频和视频数据流。最后，我们从这些数据中提取相关特征，并提出一种基于机器学习的方法来预测人类与化身对话期间的人际有效性。最后，我们讨论这些发现对于为现实世界构建有益的应用程序的影响。

AI.tldr: 开发结合人工智能与虚拟现实的框架，用于测量、预测及提升人际有效性，通过化身互动实现沉浸式学习体验。
AI.motivation: 传统体验式学习存在局限，元宇宙技术为沉浸式学习提供新途径，需探索AI与VR结合提升人际互动能力的方法。
AI.method: 融合AI与VR的架构，通过化身互动收集行为数据，结合试点与大规模实验分析数据，提取特征并构建机器学习模型预测人际有效性。
AI.result: 人际有效性不足者经多次化身互动后显著提升，行为模式接近现实，机器学习模型能有效预测人际有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架可有效提升人际互动能力，为教育、心理干预等领域提供可行的元宇宙应用方案。

authors_text: Tingting Zheng,Weixing chen,Shuqin Li,Hao Quan,Qun Bai,Tianhang Nan,Song Zheng,Xinghua Gao,Yue Zhao,Xiaoyu Cui
title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Rapid Diagnosis of Whole Slide Pathological Images
title_zh: 用于快速诊断整个幻灯片病理图像的深度强化学习框架
abstract: The deep neural network is a research hotspot for histopathological image analysis, which can improve the efficiency and accuracy of diagnosis for pathologists or be used for disease screening. The whole slide pathological image can reach one gigapixel and contains abundant tissue feature information, which needs to be divided into a lot of patches in the training and inference stages. This will lead to a long convergence time and large memory consumption. Furthermore, well-annotated data sets are also in short supply in the field of digital pathology. Inspired by the pathologist's clinical diagnosis process, we propose a weakly supervised deep reinforcement learning framework, which can greatly reduce the time required for network inference. We use neural network to construct the search model and decision model of reinforcement learning agent respectively. The search model predicts the next action through the image features of different magnifications in the current field of view, and the decision model is used to return the predicted probability of the current field of view image. In addition, an expert-guided model is constructed by multi-instance learning, which not only provides rewards for search model, but also guides decision model learning by the knowledge distillation method. Experimental results show that our proposed method can achieve fast inference and accurate prediction of whole slide images without any pixel-level annotations.
summary: The deep neural network is a research hotspot for histopathological image analysis, which can improve the efficiency and accuracy of diagnosis for pathologists or be used for disease screening. The whole slide pathological image can reach one gigapixel and contains abundant tissue feature information, which needs to be divided into a lot of patches in the training and inference stages. This will lead to a long convergence time and large memory consumption. Furthermore, well-annotated data sets are also in short supply in the field of digital pathology. Inspired by the pathologist's clinical diagnosis process, we propose a weakly supervised deep reinforcement learning framework, which can greatly reduce the time required for network inference. We use neural network to construct the search model and decision model of reinforcement learning agent respectively. The search model predicts the next action through the image features of different magnifications in the current field of view, and the decision model is used to return the predicted probability of the current field of view image. In addition, an expert-guided model is constructed by multi-instance learning, which not only provides rewards for search model, but also guides decision model learning by the knowledge distillation method. Experimental results show that our proposed method can achieve fast inference and accurate prediction of whole slide images without any pixel-level annotations.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络是组织病理学图像分析的研究热点，可以提高病理学家诊断的效率和准确性，或用于疾病筛查。整张切片病理图像可达十亿像素，包含丰富的组织特征信息，在训练和推理阶段需要将其分为很多块。这会导致收敛时间长，内存消耗大。此外，数字病理学领域也缺乏注释良好的数据集。受病理学家临床诊断过程的启发，我们提出了一种弱监督的深度强化学习框架，可以大大减少网络推理所需的时间。我们使用神经网络分别构建强化学习代理的搜索模型和决策模型。搜索模型通过当前视场不同放大倍数的图像特征来预测下一步动作，决策模型则返回当前视场图像的预测概率。此外，通过多实例学习构建专家引导模型，不仅为搜索模型提供奖励，还通过知识蒸馏的方法指导决策模型学习。实验结果表明，我们提出的方法可以实现整个幻灯片图像的快速推理和准确预测，而无需任何像素级注释。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度强化学习的弱监督框架，用于快速诊断全切片病理图像。
AI.motivation: 全切片病理图像体积庞大且标注数据稀缺，传统方法存在收敛时间长、内存消耗大等问题，需模仿病理学家诊断流程提升效率。
AI.method: 构建强化学习智能体的搜索模型与决策模型，通过多尺度图像特征预测操作并生成诊断概率，结合多实例学习的专家引导模型实现知识蒸馏与奖励机制。
AI.result: 在无需像素级标注的情况下，方法实现了全切片图像的快速推理与准确预测。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效解决了数字病理学中大规模图像处理与标注数据不足的挑战，为弱监督诊断提供了新思路。

authors_text: Khanh Nguyen,Huy Hoang Nguyen,Aleksei Tiulpin
title: AdaTriplet: Adaptive Gradient Triplet Loss with Automatic Margin Learning for Forensic Medical Image Matching
title_zh: AdaTriplet：用于法医学图像匹配的自适应梯度三重态损失和自动边缘学习
comment: 15 pages, 6 figures, accepted as a conference paper at MICCAI 2022
comments: 15 pages, 6 figures, accepted as a conference paper at MICCAI 2022
abstract: This paper tackles the challenge of forensic medical image matching (FMIM) using deep neural networks (DNNs). FMIM is a particular case of content-based image retrieval (CBIR). The main challenge in FMIM compared to the general case of CBIR, is that the subject to whom a query image belongs may be affected by aging and progressive degenerative disorders, making it difficult to match data on a subject level. CBIR with DNNs is generally solved by minimizing a ranking loss, such as Triplet loss (TL), computed on image representations extracted by a DNN from the original data. TL, in particular, operates on triplets: anchor, positive (similar to anchor) and negative (dissimilar to anchor). Although TL has been shown to perform well in many CBIR tasks, it still has limitations, which we identify and analyze in this work. In this paper, we introduce (i) the AdaTriplet loss -- an extension of TL whose gradients adapt to different difficulty levels of negative samples, and (ii) the AutoMargin method -- a technique to adjust hyperparameters of margin-based losses such as TL and our proposed loss dynamically. Our results are evaluated on two large-scale benchmarks for FMIM based on the Osteoarthritis Initiative and Chest X-ray-14 datasets. The codes allowing replication of this study have been made publicly available at \url{https://github.com/Oulu-IMEDS/AdaTriplet}.
summary: This paper tackles the challenge of forensic medical image matching (FMIM) using deep neural networks (DNNs). FMIM is a particular case of content-based image retrieval (CBIR). TL, in particular, operates on triplets: anchor, positive (similar to anchor) and negative (dissimilar to anchor). In this paper, we introduce (i) the AdaTriplet loss -- an extension of TL whose gradients adapt to different difficulty levels of negative samples, and (ii) the AutoMargin method -- a technique to adjust hyperparameters of margin-based losses such as TL and our proposed loss dynamically. Our results are evaluated on two large-scale benchmarks for FMIM based on the Osteoarthritis Initiative and Chest X-ray-14 datasets. The codes allowing replication of this study have been made publicly available at \url{https://github.com/Oulu-IMEDS/AdaTriplet}.
abstract_zh: 本文利用深度神经网络 (DNN) 解决法医医学图像匹配 (FMIM) 的挑战。 FMIM 是基于内容的图像检索 (CBIR) 的一个特例。与 CBIR 的一般情况相比，FMIM 的主要挑战是查询图像所属的主体可能会受到衰老和进行性退行性疾病的影响，从而难以匹配主体级别的数据。 DNN 的 CBIR 通常通过最小化排名损失来解决，例如三元组损失 (TL)，是根据 DNN 从原始数据提取的图像表示计算的。特别是 TL，对三元组进行操作：锚点、正值（与锚点类似）和负值（与锚点不同）。尽管 TL 已被证明在许多 CBIR 任务中表现良好，但它仍然存在局限性，我们在这项工作中识别并分析了这些局限性。在本文中，我们介绍了（i）AdaTriplet 损失——TL 的扩展，其梯度适应负样本的不同难度级别，以及（ii）AutoMargin 方法——一种动态调整基于保证金的损失（例如 TL 和我们提出的损失）的超参数的技术。我们的结果根据基于骨关节炎倡议和胸部 X 射线 14 数据集的两个大规模 FMIM 基准进行评估。允许复制这项研究的代码已在 \url{https://github.com/Oulu-IMEDS/AdaTriplet} 上公开发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AdaTriplet损失和AutoMargin方法，解决法医医学图像匹配中的挑战，提升基于深度神经网络的图像检索效果。
AI.motivation: 传统Triplet损失在处理负样本难度差异和边距参数调整上存在不足，影响法医医学图像匹配的准确性。
AI.method: 引入自适应梯度Triplet损失（AdaTriplet）和自动边距学习方法（AutoMargin），分别解决负样本难度差异和边距参数调整问题。
AI.result: 在Osteoarthritis Initiative和Chest X-ray-14数据集上的实验表明，AdaTriplet和AutoMargin显著提升了匹配准确率。
AI.conclusion: 通过自适应损失函数和自动参数调整，有效解决了FMIM中的挑战，为医学图像匹配提供了新方法。

search_text: AdaTriplet: Adaptive Gradient Triplet Loss with Automatic Margin Learning for Forensic Medical Image Matching AdaTriplet：用于法医学图像匹配的自适应梯度三重态损失和自动边缘学习 This paper tackles the challenge of forensic medical image matching (FMIM) using deep neural networks (DNNs). FMIM is a particular case of content-based image retrieval (CBIR). The main challenge in FMIM compared to the general case of CBIR, is that the subject to whom a query image belongs may be affected by aging and progressive degenerative disorders, making it difficult to match data on a subject level. CBIR with DNNs is generally solved by minimizing a ranking loss, such as Triplet loss (TL), computed on image representations extracted by a DNN from the original data. TL, in particular, operates on triplets: anchor, positive (similar to anchor) and negative (dissimilar to anchor). Although TL has been shown to perform well in many CBIR tasks, it still has limitations, which we identify and analyze in this work. In this paper, we introduce (i) the AdaTriplet loss -- an extension of TL whose gradients adapt to different difficulty levels of negative samples, and (ii) the AutoMargin method -- a technique to adjust hyperparameters of margin-based losses such as TL and our proposed loss dynamically. Our results are evaluated on two large-scale benchmarks for FMIM based on the Osteoarthritis Initiative and Chest X-ray-14 datasets. The codes allowing replication of this study have been made publicly available at \url{https://github.com/Oulu-IMEDS/AdaTriplet}. 本文利用深度神经网络 (DNN) 解决法医医学图像匹配 (FMIM) 的挑战。 FMIM 是基于内容的图像检索 (CBIR) 的一个特例。与 CBIR 的一般情况相比，FMIM 的主要挑战是查询图像所属的主体可能会受到衰老和进行性退行性疾病的影响，从而难以匹配主体级别的数据。 DNN 的 CBIR 通常通过最小化排名损失来解决，例如三元组损失 (TL)，是根据 DNN 从原始数据提取的图像表示计算的。特别是 TL，对三元组进行操作：锚点、正值（与锚点类似）和负值（与锚点不同）。尽管 TL 已被证明在许多 CBIR 任务中表现良好，但它仍然存在局限性，我们在这项工作中识别并分析了这些局限性。在本文中，我们介绍了（i）AdaTriplet 损失——TL 的扩展，其梯度适应负样本的不同难度级别，以及（ii）AutoMargin 方法——一种动态调整基于保证金的损失（例如 TL 和我们提出的损失）的超参数的技术。我们的结果根据基于骨关节炎倡议和胸部 X 射线 14 数据集的两个大规模 FMIM 基准进行评估。允许复制这项研究的代码已在 \url{https://github.com/Oulu-IMEDS/AdaTriplet} 上公开发布。 本文提出AdaTriplet损失和AutoMargin方法，解决法医医学图像匹配中的挑战，提升基于深度神经网络的图像检索效果。 Khanh Nguyen,Huy Hoang Nguyen,Aleksei Tiulpin eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Florian Thamm,Oliver Taubmann,Markus Jürgens,Aleksandra Thamm,Felix Denzinger,Leonhard Rist,Hendrik Ditt,Andreas Maier
title: Building Brains: Subvolume Recombination for Data Augmentation in Large Vessel Occlusion Detection
title_zh: 构建大脑：大血管闭塞检测中数据增强的子体积重组
abstract: Ischemic strokes are often caused by large vessel occlusions (LVOs), which can be visualized and diagnosed with Computed Tomography Angiography scans. As time is brain, a fast, accurate and automated diagnosis of these scans is desirable. Human readers compare the left and right hemispheres in their assessment of strokes. A large training data set is required for a standard deep learning-based model to learn this strategy from data. As labeled medical data in this field is rare, other approaches need to be developed. To both include the prior knowledge of side comparison and increase the amount of training data, we propose an augmentation method that generates artificial training samples by recombining vessel tree segmentations of the hemispheres or hemisphere subregions from different patients. The subregions cover vessels commonly affected by LVOs, namely the internal carotid artery (ICA) and middle cerebral artery (MCA). In line with the augmentation scheme, we use a 3D-DenseNet fed with task-specific input, fostering a side-by-side comparison between the hemispheres. Furthermore, we propose an extension of that architecture to process the individual hemisphere subregions. All configurations predict the presence of an LVO, its side, and the affected subregion. We show the effect of recombination as an augmentation strategy in a 5-fold cross validated ablation study. We enhanced the AUC for patient-wise classification regarding the presence of an LVO of all investigated architectures. For one variant, the proposed method improved the AUC from 0.73 without augmentation to 0.89. The best configuration detects LVOs with an AUC of 0.91, LVOs in the ICA with an AUC of 0.96, and in the MCA with 0.91 while accurately predicting the affected side.
summary: Ischemic strokes are often caused by large vessel occlusions (LVOs), which can be visualized and diagnosed with Computed Tomography Angiography scans. As time is brain, a fast, accurate and automated diagnosis of these scans is desirable. Human readers compare the left and right hemispheres in their assessment of strokes. A large training data set is required for a standard deep learning-based model to learn this strategy from data. As labeled medical data in this field is rare, other approaches need to be developed. To both include the prior knowledge of side comparison and increase the amount of training data, we propose an augmentation method that generates artificial training samples by recombining vessel tree segmentations of the hemispheres or hemisphere subregions from different patients. The subregions cover vessels commonly affected by LVOs, namely the internal carotid artery (ICA) and middle cerebral artery (MCA). In line with the augmentation scheme, we use a 3D-DenseNet fed with task-specific input, fostering a side-by-side comparison between the hemispheres. Furthermore, we propose an extension of that architecture to process the individual hemisphere subregions. All configurations predict the presence of an LVO, its side, and the affected subregion. We show the effect of recombination as an augmentation strategy in a 5-fold cross validated ablation study. We enhanced the AUC for patient-wise classification regarding the presence of an LVO of all investigated architectures. For one variant, the proposed method improved the AUC from 0.73 without augmentation to 0.89. The best configuration detects LVOs with an AUC of 0.91, LVOs in the ICA with an AUC of 0.96, and in the MCA with 0.91 while accurately predicting the affected side.
abstract_zh: 缺血性中风通常由大血管闭塞 (LVO) 引起，可以通过计算机断层扫描血管造影扫描进行可视化和诊断。由于时间就是大脑，因此需要对这些扫描进行快速、准确和自动的诊断。人类读者在评估中风时会比较左右半球。基于标准深度学习的模型需要大量训练数据集才能从数据中学习该策略。由于该领域的标记医疗数据很少，因此需要开发其他方法。为了既包含侧面比较的先验知识并增加训练数据量，我们提出了一种增强方法，通过重新组合来自不同患者的半球或半球子区域的血管树分割来生成人工训练样本。这些分区覆盖了通常受 LVO 影响的血管，即颈内动脉 (ICA) 和大脑中动脉 (MCA)。根据增强方案，我们使用 3D-DenseNet 来提供特定于任务的输入，从而促进半球之间的并排比较。此外，我们建议对该架构进行扩展以处理各个半球子区域。所有配置都预测 LVO、其侧面和受影响的子区域的存在。我们在五倍交叉验证的消融研究中展示了重组作为增强策略的效果。我们增强了关于所有研究架构中是否存在 LVO 的患者分类的 AUC。对于一种变体，所提出的方法将 AUC 从没有增强的 0.73 提高到 0.89。最佳配置检测 AUC 为 0.91 的 LVO、AUC 为 0.96 的 ICA 中的 LVO、AUC 为 0.91 的 MCA 中的 LVO，同时准确预测受影响的一侧。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过重组患者半球或亚区血管分割数据来增强训练数据的方法，用于提高大血管闭塞检测的深度学习模型性能。
AI.motivation: 由于标注的医学数据稀缺，且人类医生通过比较左右半球诊断中风，需开发结合先验知识并增加训练数据的方法以提升模型准确性。
AI.method: 采用3D-DenseNet网络，通过重组不同患者半球或亚区（如ICA、MCA）的血管分割数据生成人工样本，并扩展网络结构以处理亚区信息。
AI.result: 在五折交叉验证中，所提方法将患者级LVO检测AUC从0.73提升至0.89，最佳配置在ICA和MCA检测中分别达到0.96和0.91的AUC。
AI.conclusion: 子体积重组增强策略有效提升了模型对LVO存在性及侧别的检测能力，尤其在关键血管区域表现优异。

authors_text: Ikboljon Sobirov,Numan Saeed,Mohammad Yaqub
title: Super Images -- A New 2D Perspective on 3D Medical Imaging Analysis
title_zh: 超级图像——3D医学影像分析的2D新视角
abstract: In medical imaging analysis, deep learning has shown promising results. We frequently rely on volumetric data to segment medical images, necessitating the use of 3D architectures, which are commended for their capacity to capture interslice context. However, because of the 3D convolutions, max pooling, up-convolutions, and other operations utilized in these networks, these architectures are often more inefficient in terms of time and computation than their 2D equivalents. Furthermore, there are few 3D pretrained model weights, and pretraining is often difficult. We present a simple yet effective 2D method to handle 3D data while efficiently embedding the 3D knowledge during training. We propose transforming volumetric data into 2D super images and segmenting with 2D networks to solve these challenges. Our method generates a super-resolution image by stitching slices side by side in the 3D image. We expect deep neural networks to capture and learn these properties spatially despite losing depth information. This work aims to present a novel perspective when dealing with volumetric data, and we test the hypothesis using CNN and ViT networks as well as self-supervised pretraining. While attaining equal, if not superior, results to 3D networks utilizing only 2D counterparts, the model complexity is reduced by around threefold. Because volumetric data is relatively scarce, we anticipate that our approach will entice more studies, particularly in medical imaging analysis.
summary: In medical imaging analysis, deep learning has shown promising results. We frequently rely on volumetric data to segment medical images, necessitating the use of 3D architectures, which are commended for their capacity to capture interslice context. However, because of the 3D convolutions, max pooling, up-convolutions, and other operations utilized in these networks, these architectures are often more inefficient in terms of time and computation than their 2D equivalents. Furthermore, there are few 3D pretrained model weights, and pretraining is often difficult. We present a simple yet effective 2D method to handle 3D data while efficiently embedding the 3D knowledge during training. We propose transforming volumetric data into 2D super images and segmenting with 2D networks to solve these challenges. Our method generates a super-resolution image by stitching slices side by side in the 3D image. We expect deep neural networks to capture and learn these properties spatially despite losing depth information. This work aims to present a novel perspective when dealing with volumetric data, and we test the hypothesis using CNN and ViT networks as well as self-supervised pretraining. While attaining equal, if not superior, results to 3D networks utilizing only 2D counterparts, the model complexity is reduced by around threefold. Because volumetric data is relatively scarce, we anticipate that our approach will entice more studies, particularly in medical imaging analysis.
abstract_zh: 在医学影像分析中，深度学习已经显示出有希望的结果。我们经常依赖体积数据来分割医学图像，因此需要使用 3D 架构，而 3D 架构因其捕获切片间上下文的能力而受到赞扬。然而，由于这些网络中使用了 3D 卷积、最大池化、上卷积和其他操作，这些架构在时间和计算方面通常比 2D 等效架构效率更低。此外，3D预训练模型权重很少，预训练通常很困难。我们提出了一种简单而有效的 2D 方法来处理 3D 数据，同时在训练期间有效地嵌入 3D 知识。我们建议将体积数据转换为 2D 超级图像并使用 2D 网络进行分割来解决这些挑战。我们的方法通过在 3D 图像中并排拼接切片来生成超分辨率图像。我们期望深度神经网络能够在丢失深度信息的情况下捕获和学习这些空间属性。这项工作旨在在处理体积数据时提出一种新颖的视角，我们使用 CNN 和 ViT 网络以及自监督预训练来测试这一假设。虽然获得与仅使用 2D 对应物的 3D 网络相同（如果不是更好）的结果，但模型复杂性降低了大约三倍。由于体积数据相对稀缺，我们预计我们的方法将吸引更多的研究，特别是在医学成像分析方面。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种将3D医学影像转换为2D超图像的新方法，利用2D网络高效处理体积数据。
AI.motivation: 3D网络虽能捕捉切片间上下文，但计算效率低且预训练模型稀缺，亟需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过拼接3D影像切片生成高分辨率2D超图像，采用CNN和ViT网络结合自监督预训练进行分割。
AI.result: 2D方法在保持效果的同时降低模型复杂度约三倍，部分指标超越3D网络。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为医学影像分析提供新视角，有望推动该领域研究发展。

search_text: Super Images -- A New 2D Perspective on 3D Medical Imaging Analysis 超级图像——3D医学影像分析的2D新视角 In medical imaging analysis, deep learning has shown promising results. We frequently rely on volumetric data to segment medical images, necessitating the use of 3D architectures, which are commended for their capacity to capture interslice context. However, because of the 3D convolutions, max pooling, up-convolutions, and other operations utilized in these networks, these architectures are often more inefficient in terms of time and computation than their 2D equivalents. Furthermore, there are few 3D pretrained model weights, and pretraining is often difficult. We present a simple yet effective 2D method to handle 3D data while efficiently embedding the 3D knowledge during training. We propose transforming volumetric data into 2D super images and segmenting with 2D networks to solve these challenges. Our method generates a super-resolution image by stitching slices side by side in the 3D image. We expect deep neural networks to capture and learn these properties spatially despite losing depth information. This work aims to present a novel perspective when dealing with volumetric data, and we test the hypothesis using CNN and ViT networks as well as self-supervised pretraining. While attaining equal, if not superior, results to 3D networks utilizing only 2D counterparts, the model complexity is reduced by around threefold. Because volumetric data is relatively scarce, we anticipate that our approach will entice more studies, particularly in medical imaging analysis. 在医学影像分析中，深度学习已经显示出有希望的结果。我们经常依赖体积数据来分割医学图像，因此需要使用 3D 架构，而 3D 架构因其捕获切片间上下文的能力而受到赞扬。然而，由于这些网络中使用了 3D 卷积、最大池化、上卷积和其他操作，这些架构在时间和计算方面通常比 2D 等效架构效率更低。此外，3D预训练模型权重很少，预训练通常很困难。我们提出了一种简单而有效的 2D 方法来处理 3D 数据，同时在训练期间有效地嵌入 3D 知识。我们建议将体积数据转换为 2D 超级图像并使用 2D 网络进行分割来解决这些挑战。我们的方法通过在 3D 图像中并排拼接切片来生成超分辨率图像。我们期望深度神经网络能够在丢失深度信息的情况下捕获和学习这些空间属性。这项工作旨在在处理体积数据时提出一种新颖的视角，我们使用 CNN 和 ViT 网络以及自监督预训练来测试这一假设。虽然获得与仅使用 2D 对应物的 3D 网络相同（如果不是更好）的结果，但模型复杂性降低了大约三倍。由于体积数据相对稀缺，我们预计我们的方法将吸引更多的研究，特别是在医学成像分析方面。 本文提出一种将3D医学影像转换为2D超图像的新方法，利用2D网络高效处理体积数据。 Ikboljon Sobirov,Numan Saeed,Mohammad Yaqub eess.IV,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Yuxin Kang,Hansheng Li,Xuan Zhao,Dongqing Hu,Feihong Liu,Lei Cui,Jun Feng,Lin Yang
title: Invariant Content Synergistic Learning for Domain Generalization of Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 医学图像分割领域泛化的不变内容协同学习
abstract: While achieving remarkable success for medical image segmentation, deep convolution neural networks (DCNNs) often fail to maintain their robustness when confronting test data with the novel distribution. To address such a drawback, the inductive bias of DCNNs is recently well-recognized. Specifically, DCNNs exhibit an inductive bias towards image style (e.g., superficial texture) rather than invariant content (e.g., object shapes). In this paper, we propose a method, named Invariant Content Synergistic Learning (ICSL), to improve the generalization ability of DCNNs on unseen datasets by controlling the inductive bias. First, ICSL mixes the style of training instances to perturb the training distribution. That is to say, more diverse domains or styles would be made available for training DCNNs. Based on the perturbed distribution, we carefully design a dual-branches invariant content synergistic learning strategy to prevent style-biased predictions and focus more on the invariant content. Extensive experimental results on two typical medical image segmentation tasks show that our approach performs better than state-of-the-art domain generalization methods.
summary: While achieving remarkable success for medical image segmentation, deep convolution neural networks (DCNNs) often fail to maintain their robustness when confronting test data with the novel distribution. To address such a drawback, the inductive bias of DCNNs is recently well-recognized. Specifically, DCNNs exhibit an inductive bias towards image style (e.g., superficial texture) rather than invariant content (e.g., object shapes). In this paper, we propose a method, named Invariant Content Synergistic Learning (ICSL), to improve the generalization ability of DCNNs on unseen datasets by controlling the inductive bias. First, ICSL mixes the style of training instances to perturb the training distribution. That is to say, more diverse domains or styles would be made available for training DCNNs. Based on the perturbed distribution, we carefully design a dual-branches invariant content synergistic learning strategy to prevent style-biased predictions and focus more on the invariant content. Extensive experimental results on two typical medical image segmentation tasks show that our approach performs better than state-of-the-art domain generalization methods.
abstract_zh: 虽然在医学图像分割方面取得了显着的成功，但深度卷积神经网络 (DCNN) 在面对具有新分布的测试数据时往往无法保持其鲁棒性。为了解决这个缺点，DCNN 的归纳偏差最近得到了广泛的认可。具体来说，DCNN 对图像风格（例如，表面纹理）而不是不变内容（例如，对象形状）表现出归纳偏差。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为不变内容协同学习（ICSL）的方法，通过控制归纳偏差来提高 DCNN 在未见过的数据集上的泛化能力。首先，ICSL 混合训练实例的风格来扰乱训练分布。也就是说，将有更多不同的领域或风格可用于训练 DCNN。基于扰动分布，我们精心设计了双分支不变内容协同学习策略，以防止风格偏见的预测并更多地关注不变内容。对两种典型医学图像分割任务的广泛实验结果表明，我们的方法比最先进的领域泛化方法表现更好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为ICSL的方法，通过控制深度卷积神经网络的归纳偏置，提升其在未见医学图像分割数据集上的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 深度卷积神经网络在医学图像分割中易受测试数据分布变化影响，因其过度关注图像风格（如纹理）而非不变内容（如形状），导致鲁棒性不足。
AI.method: ICSL通过混合训练样本的风格扰动分布，并设计双分支协同学习策略，抑制风格偏差以聚焦不变内容。
AI.result: 在两个典型医学图像分割任务中，ICSL优于现有最先进的领域泛化方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过调整归纳偏置使网络关注不变内容，ICSL有效提升了医学图像分割模型的领域泛化能力。

authors_text: Ethan Schonfeld,Anand Veeravagu
title: Generative Adversarial Network Based Synthetic Learning and a Novel Domain Relevant Loss Term for Spine Radiographs
title_zh: 基于生成对抗网络的综合学习和脊柱射线照片的新领域相关损失项
abstract: Problem: There is a lack of big data for the training of deep learning models in medicine, characterized by the time cost of data collection and privacy concerns. Generative adversarial networks (GANs) offer both the potential to generate new data, as well as to use this newly generated data, without inclusion of patients' real data, for downstream applications. Approach: A series of GANs were trained and applied for a downstream computer vision spine radiograph abnormality classification task. Separate classifiers were trained with either access or no access to the original imaging. Trained GANs included a conditional StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation, a conditional StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation to generate spine radiographs conditional on lesion type, and using a novel clinical loss term for the generator a StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation conditional on abnormality (SpineGAN). Finally, a differential privacy imposed StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation conditional on abnormality was trained and an ablation study was performed on its differential privacy impositions. Key Results: We accomplish GAN generation of synthetic spine radiographs without meaningful input for the first time from a literature review. We further demonstrate the success of synthetic learning for the spine domain with a downstream clinical classification task (AUC of 0.830 using synthetic data compared to AUC of 0.886 using the real data). Importantly, the introduction of a new clinical loss term for the generator was found to increase generation recall as well as accelerate model training. Lastly, we demonstrate that, in a limited size medical dataset, differential privacy impositions severely impede GAN training, finding that this is specifically due to the requirement for gradient perturbation with noise.
summary: Problem: There is a lack of big data for the training of deep learning models in medicine, characterized by the time cost of data collection and privacy concerns. Generative adversarial networks (GANs) offer both the potential to generate new data, as well as to use this newly generated data, without inclusion of patients' real data, for downstream applications. Approach: A series of GANs were trained and applied for a downstream computer vision spine radiograph abnormality classification task. Separate classifiers were trained with either access or no access to the original imaging. Trained GANs included a conditional StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation, a conditional StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation to generate spine radiographs conditional on lesion type, and using a novel clinical loss term for the generator a StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation conditional on abnormality (SpineGAN). Finally, a differential privacy imposed StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation conditional on abnormality was trained and an ablation study was performed on its differential privacy impositions. Key Results: We accomplish GAN generation of synthetic spine radiographs without meaningful input for the first time from a literature review. We further demonstrate the success of synthetic learning for the spine domain with a downstream clinical classification task (AUC of 0.830 using synthetic data compared to AUC of 0.886 using the real data). Importantly, the introduction of a new clinical loss term for the generator was found to increase generation recall as well as accelerate model training. Lastly, we demonstrate that, in a limited size medical dataset, differential privacy impositions severely impede GAN training, finding that this is specifically due to the requirement for gradient perturbation with noise.
abstract_zh: 问题：缺乏用于医学深度学习模型训练的大数据，其特点是数据收集的时间成本和隐私问题。生成对抗网络（GAN）既提供了生成新数据的潜力，也提供了使用这些新生成的数据（不包含患者的真实数据）进行下游应用的潜力。方法：对一系列 GAN 进行训练并应用于下游计算机视觉脊柱射线照片异常分类任务。单独的分类器在访问或不访问原始图像的情况下进行训练。经过训练的 GAN 包括具有自适应判别器增强功能的条件 StyleGAN2、具有自适应判别器增强功能以​​根据病变类型生成脊柱 X 光照片的条件 StyleGAN2，以及使用一种新颖的临床损失项生成器，具有以异常为条件的自适应判别器增强功能的 StyleGAN2 (SpineGAN)。最后，训练了具有异常情况下自适应判别器增强功能的差分隐私强加 StyleGAN2，并对其差分隐私强加进行了消融研究。主要结果：我们首次在没有文献综述的有意义输入的情况下完成了合成脊柱射线照片的 GAN 生成。我们通过下游临床分类任务进一步证明了脊柱领域综合学习的成功（使用合成数据的 AUC 为 0.830，而使用真实数据的 AUC 为 0.886）。重要的是，为生成器引入新的临床损失项被发现可以增加生成回忆并加速模型训练。最后，我们证明，在有限大小的医学数据集中，差分隐私强制严重阻碍了 GAN 训练，发现这具体是由于噪声梯度扰动的要求。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的合成学习方法，并引入新的领域相关损失项，用于脊柱X光片的异常分类任务。
AI.motivation: 医学领域缺乏大规模标注数据，且数据收集耗时且涉及隐私问题。GAN可通过生成合成数据解决这一问题，同时避免使用真实患者数据。
AI.method: 训练多种改进的StyleGAN2模型（含自适应判别器增强），包括条件生成脊柱X光片的模型及引入新型临床损失项的SpineGAN。通过对比实验验证合成数据在分类任务中的有效性，并分析差分隐私对训练的影响。
AI.result: 首次实现无需明确输入的脊柱X光片合成生成，合成数据在分类任务中达到AUC 0.83（真实数据为0.886）。新型损失项提升了生成召回率并加速训练，但差分隐私机制因梯度噪声显著阻碍训练。
AI.conclusion: 合成学习在医学影像领域具有潜力，新型损失项可优化生成效果，但差分隐私在小数据集下可能影响GAN训练稳定性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的合成学习方法，并引入新的领域相关损失项，用于脊柱X光片的异常分类任务。
ai_content.motivation: 医学领域缺乏大规模标注数据，且数据收集耗时且涉及隐私问题。GAN可通过生成合成数据解决这一问题，同时避免使用真实患者数据。
ai_content.method: 训练多种改进的StyleGAN2模型（含自适应判别器增强），包括条件生成脊柱X光片的模型及引入新型临床损失项的SpineGAN。通过对比实验验证合成数据在分类任务中的有效性，并分析差分隐私对训练的影响。
ai_content.result: 首次实现无需明确输入的脊柱X光片合成生成，合成数据在分类任务中达到AUC 0.83（真实数据为0.886）。新型损失项提升了生成召回率并加速训练，但差分隐私机制因梯度噪声显著阻碍训练。
ai_content.conclusion: 合成学习在医学影像领域具有潜力，新型损失项可优化生成效果，但差分隐私在小数据集下可能影响GAN训练稳定性。

search_text: Generative Adversarial Network Based Synthetic Learning and a Novel Domain Relevant Loss Term for Spine Radiographs 基于生成对抗网络的综合学习和脊柱射线照片的新领域相关损失项 Problem: There is a lack of big data for the training of deep learning models in medicine, characterized by the time cost of data collection and privacy concerns. Generative adversarial networks (GANs) offer both the potential to generate new data, as well as to use this newly generated data, without inclusion of patients' real data, for downstream applications. Approach: A series of GANs were trained and applied for a downstream computer vision spine radiograph abnormality classification task. Separate classifiers were trained with either access or no access to the original imaging. Trained GANs included a conditional StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation, a conditional StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation to generate spine radiographs conditional on lesion type, and using a novel clinical loss term for the generator a StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation conditional on abnormality (SpineGAN). Finally, a differential privacy imposed StyleGAN2 with adaptive discriminator augmentation conditional on abnormality was trained and an ablation study was performed on its differential privacy impositions. Key Results: We accomplish GAN generation of synthetic spine radiographs without meaningful input for the first time from a literature review. We further demonstrate the success of synthetic learning for the spine domain with a downstream clinical classification task (AUC of 0.830 using synthetic data compared to AUC of 0.886 using the real data). Importantly, the introduction of a new clinical loss term for the generator was found to increase generation recall as well as accelerate model training. Lastly, we demonstrate that, in a limited size medical dataset, differential privacy impositions severely impede GAN training, finding that this is specifically due to the requirement for gradient perturbation with noise. 问题：缺乏用于医学深度学习模型训练的大数据，其特点是数据收集的时间成本和隐私问题。生成对抗网络（GAN）既提供了生成新数据的潜力，也提供了使用这些新生成的数据（不包含患者的真实数据）进行下游应用的潜力。方法：对一系列 GAN 进行训练并应用于下游计算机视觉脊柱射线照片异常分类任务。单独的分类器在访问或不访问原始图像的情况下进行训练。经过训练的 GAN 包括具有自适应判别器增强功能的条件 StyleGAN2、具有自适应判别器增强功能以​​根据病变类型生成脊柱 X 光照片的条件 StyleGAN2，以及使用一种新颖的临床损失项生成器，具有以异常为条件的自适应判别器增强功能的 StyleGAN2 (SpineGAN)。最后，训练了具有异常情况下自适应判别器增强功能的差分隐私强加 StyleGAN2，并对其差分隐私强加进行了消融研究。主要结果：我们首次在没有文献综述的有意义输入的情况下完成了合成脊柱射线照片的 GAN 生成。我们通过下游临床分类任务进一步证明了脊柱领域综合学习的成功（使用合成数据的 AUC 为 0.830，而使用真实数据的 AUC 为 0.886）。重要的是，为生成器引入新的临床损失项被发现可以增加生成回忆并加速模型训练。最后，我们证明，在有限大小的医学数据集中，差分隐私强制严重阻碍了 GAN 训练，发现这具体是由于噪声梯度扰动的要求。 本文提出基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的合成学习方法，并引入新的领域相关损失项，用于脊柱X光片的异常分类任务。 Ethan Schonfeld,Anand Veeravagu eess.IV,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Weichen Fan,Yuanbo Yang,Kunpeng Qiu,Shuo Wang,Yongxin Guo
title: InvNorm: Domain Generalization for Object Detection in Gastrointestinal Endoscopy
title_zh: InvNorm：胃肠内窥镜中物体检测的领域概括
abstract: Domain Generalization is a challenging topic in computer vision, especially in Gastrointestinal Endoscopy image analysis. Due to several device limitations and ethical reasons, current open-source datasets are typically collected on a limited number of patients using the same brand of sensors. Different brands of devices and individual differences will significantly affect the model's generalizability. Therefore, to address the generalization problem in GI(Gastrointestinal) endoscopy, we propose a multi-domain GI dataset and a light, plug-in block called InvNorm(Invertible Normalization), which could achieve a better generalization performance in any structure. Previous DG(Domain Generalization) methods fail to achieve invertible transformation, which would lead to some misleading augmentation. Moreover, these models would be more likely to lead to medical ethics issues. Our method utilizes normalizing flow to achieve invertible and explainable style normalization to address the problem. The effectiveness of InvNorm is demonstrated on a wide range of tasks, including GI recognition, GI object detection, and natural image recognition.
summary: Domain Generalization is a challenging topic in computer vision, especially in Gastrointestinal Endoscopy image analysis. Due to several device limitations and ethical reasons, current open-source datasets are typically collected on a limited number of patients using the same brand of sensors. Different brands of devices and individual differences will significantly affect the model's generalizability. Therefore, to address the generalization problem in GI(Gastrointestinal) endoscopy, we propose a multi-domain GI dataset and a light, plug-in block called InvNorm(Invertible Normalization), which could achieve a better generalization performance in any structure. Previous DG(Domain Generalization) methods fail to achieve invertible transformation, which would lead to some misleading augmentation. Moreover, these models would be more likely to lead to medical ethics issues. Our method utilizes normalizing flow to achieve invertible and explainable style normalization to address the problem. The effectiveness of InvNorm is demonstrated on a wide range of tasks, including GI recognition, GI object detection, and natural image recognition.
abstract_zh: 域泛化是计算机视觉中的一个具有挑战性的主题，特别是在胃肠内窥镜图像分析中。由于多种设备限制和道德原因，当前的开源数据集通常是使用同一品牌的传感器收集有限数量的患者。不同品牌的设备和个体差异会显着影响模型的普适性。因此，为了解决 GI（胃肠）内窥镜检查中的泛化问题，我们提出了一个多域 GI 数据集和一个名为 InvNorm（Invertible Normalization）的轻型插件块，它可以在任何结构中实现更好的泛化性能。以前的DG（Domain Generalization）方法无法实现可逆变换，这会导致一些误导性的增强。此外，这些模型更有可能导致医学伦理问题。我们的方法利用规范化流来实现可逆且可解释的风格规范化来解决该问题。 InvNorm 的有效性在广泛的任务中得到了证明，包括 GI 识别、GI 对象检测和自然图像识别。

AI.tldr: 提出InvNorm方法和多领域GI数据集，解决胃肠道内镜图像分析中的领域泛化问题，提升模型在不同设备和个体间的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有公开数据集因设备品牌和患者群体局限，导致模型泛化能力不足，不同设备差异和个体差异显著影响检测效果，需更通用的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计轻量可插拔模块InvNorm，基于归一化流实现可逆且可解释的风格归一化，解决传统领域泛化方法不可逆变换导致的增强误导问题。
AI.result: InvNorm在GI图像识别、目标检测及自然图像任务中均展现优越泛化性能，验证了其跨领域适应性和有效性。
AI.conclusion: InvNorm通过可逆归一化机制有效提升模型泛化能力，为医疗图像分析中的领域泛化问题提供创新解决方案。

Due to several device limitations and ethical reasons, current open-source datasets are typically collected on a limited number of patients using the same brand of sensors. authors_text: Edward Vendrow,Ethan Schonfeld
title: Understanding Transfer Learning for Chest Radiograph Clinical Report Generation with Modified Transformer Architectures
title_zh: 了解使用改进的 Transformer 架构生成胸部 X 光片临床报告的迁移学习
abstract: The image captioning task is increasingly prevalent in artificial intelligence applications for medicine. One important application is clinical report generation from chest radiographs. The clinical writing of unstructured reports is time consuming and error-prone. An automated system would improve standardization, error reduction, time consumption, and medical accessibility. In this paper we demonstrate the importance of domain specific pre-training and propose a modified transformer architecture for the medical image captioning task. To accomplish this, we train a series of modified transformers to generate clinical reports from chest radiograph image input. These modified transformers include: a meshed-memory augmented transformer architecture with visual extractor using ImageNet pre-trained weights, a meshed-memory augmented transformer architecture with visual extractor using CheXpert pre-trained weights, and a meshed-memory augmented transformer whose encoder is passed the concatenated embeddings using both ImageNet pre-trained weights and CheXpert pre-trained weights. We use BLEU(1-4), ROUGE-L, CIDEr, and the clinical CheXbert F1 scores to validate our models and demonstrate competitive scores with state of the art models. We provide evidence that ImageNet pre-training is ill-suited for the medical image captioning task, especially for less frequent conditions (eg: enlarged cardiomediastinum, lung lesion, pneumothorax). Furthermore, we demonstrate that the double feature model improves performance for specific medical conditions (edema, consolidation, pneumothorax, support devices) and overall CheXbert F1 score, and should be further developed in future work. Such a double feature model, including both ImageNet pre-training as well as domain specific pre-training, could be used in a wide range of image captioning models in medicine.
summary: The image captioning task is increasingly prevalent in artificial intelligence applications for medicine. One important application is clinical report generation from chest radiographs. The clinical writing of unstructured reports is time consuming and error-prone. An automated system would improve standardization, error reduction, time consumption, and medical accessibility. In this paper we demonstrate the importance of domain specific pre-training and propose a modified transformer architecture for the medical image captioning task. To accomplish this, we train a series of modified transformers to generate clinical reports from chest radiograph image input. These modified transformers include: a meshed-memory augmented transformer architecture with visual extractor using ImageNet pre-trained weights, a meshed-memory augmented transformer architecture with visual extractor using CheXpert pre-trained weights, and a meshed-memory augmented transformer whose encoder is passed the concatenated embeddings using both ImageNet pre-trained weights and CheXpert pre-trained weights. We use BLEU(1-4), ROUGE-L, CIDEr, and the clinical CheXbert F1 scores to validate our models and demonstrate competitive scores with state of the art models. We provide evidence that ImageNet pre-training is ill-suited for the medical image captioning task, especially for less frequent conditions (eg: enlarged cardiomediastinum, lung lesion, pneumothorax). Furthermore, we demonstrate that the double feature model improves performance for specific medical conditions (edema, consolidation, pneumothorax, support devices) and overall CheXbert F1 score, and should be further developed in future work. Such a double feature model, including both ImageNet pre-training as well as domain specific pre-training, could be used in a wide range of image captioning models in medicine.
abstract_zh: 图像字幕任务在医学人工智能应用中越来越普遍。一项重要的应用是根据胸片生成临床报告。非结构化报告的临床写作既耗时又容易出错。自动化系统将提高标准化、减少错误、缩短时间并提高医疗可及性。在本文中，我们证明了特定领域预训练的重要性，并为医学图像字幕任务提出了一种改进的变压器架构。为了实现这一目标，我们训练了一系列经过修改的变压器，以根据胸片图像输入生成临床报告。这些修改后的变压器包括：带有使用 ImageNet 预训练权重的视觉提取器的网格内存增强变压器架构、带有使用 CheXpert 预训练权重的视觉提取器的网格内存增强变压器架构，以及一个网格内存增强变压器，其编码器使用 ImageNet 预训练权重和 CheXpert 预训练权重传递级联嵌入。我们使用 BLEU(1-4)、ROUGE-L、CIDEr 和临床 CheXbert F1 分数来验证我们的模型，并展示与最先进模型的竞争分数。我们提供的证据表明 ImageNet 预训练不适合医学图像字幕任务，特别是对于不太常见的情况（例如：扩大的心纵隔、肺部病变、气胸）。此外，我们证明双特征模型可以提高特定医疗状况（水肿、实变、气胸、支持设备）和整体 CheXbert F1 评分的性能，并且应该在未来的工作中进一步开发。这种双特征模型，包括 ImageNet 预训练和特定领域预训练，可广泛用于医学中的图像字幕模型。

AI.tldr: 本文研究使用改进的Transformer架构进行胸部X光临床报告生成，并探讨迁移学习在医学图像字幕任务中的效果。
AI.motivation: 手动撰写胸部X光临床报告耗时且易出错，自动化系统可提升标准化和效率，但现有模型在医学特定任务上表现不足，需探索更有效的迁移学习方法。
AI.method: 设计三种改进的Transformer模型：基于ImageNet预训练的视觉提取器、基于CheXpert预训练的视觉提取器，以及结合两者嵌入的双特征模型，通过BLEU、ROUGE-L、CIDEr和CheXbert F1分数进行评估。
AI.result: ImageNet预训练在低频疾病（如心影增大、肺部病变）表现不佳，双特征模型在水肿、实变、气胸等特定疾病及整体CheXbert F1分数上显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 双特征模型（结合ImageNet与领域预训练）在医学图像字幕任务中效果更优，应进一步开发此类模型以提升医疗AI的准确性与适用性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文研究使用改进的Transformer架构进行胸部X光临床报告生成，并探讨迁移学习在医学图像字幕任务中的效果。
ai_content.motivation: 手动撰写胸部X光临床报告耗时且易出错，自动化系统可提升标准化和效率，但现有模型在医学特定任务上表现不足，需探索更有效的迁移学习方法。
ai_content.method: 设计三种改进的Transformer模型：基于ImageNet预训练的视觉提取器、基于CheXpert预训练的视觉提取器，以及结合两者嵌入的双特征模型，通过BLEU、ROUGE-L、CIDEr和CheXbert F1分数进行评估。
ai_content.result: ImageNet预训练在低频疾病（如心影增大、肺部病变）表现不佳，双特征模型在水肿、实变、气胸等特定疾病及整体CheXbert F1分数上显著提升。
ai_content.conclusion: 双特征模型（结合ImageNet与领域预训练）在医学图像字幕任务中效果更优，应进一步开发此类模型以提升医疗AI的准确性与适用性。

search_text: Understanding Transfer Learning for Chest Radiograph Clinical Report Generation with Modified Transformer Architectures 了解使用改进的 Transformer 架构生成胸部 X 光片临床报告的迁移学习 The image captioning task is increasingly prevalent in artificial intelligence applications for medicine. One important application is clinical report generation from chest radiographs. The clinical writing of unstructured reports is time consuming and error-prone. An automated system would improve standardization, error reduction, time consumption, and medical accessibility. In this paper we demonstrate the importance of domain specific pre-training and propose a modified transformer architecture for the medical image captioning task. To accomplish this, we train a series of modified transformers to generate clinical reports from chest radiograph image input. These modified transformers include: a meshed-memory augmented transformer architecture with visual extractor using ImageNet pre-trained weights, a meshed-memory augmented transformer architecture with visual extractor using CheXpert pre-trained weights, and a meshed-memory augmented transformer whose encoder is passed the concatenated embeddings using both ImageNet pre-trained weights and CheXpert pre-trained weights. We use BLEU(1-4), ROUGE-L, CIDEr, and the clinical CheXbert F1 scores to validate our models and demonstrate competitive scores with state of the art models. We provide evidence that ImageNet pre-training is ill-suited for the medical image captioning task, especially for less frequent conditions (eg: enlarged cardiomediastinum, lung lesion, pneumothorax). Furthermore, we demonstrate that the double feature model improves performance for specific medical conditions (edema, consolidation, pneumothorax, support devices) and overall CheXbert F1 score, and should be further developed in future work. Such a double feature model, including both ImageNet pre-training as well as domain specific pre-training, could be used in a wide range of image captioning models in medicine. 图像字幕任务在医学人工智能应用中越来越普遍。一项重要的应用是根据胸片生成临床报告。非结构化报告的临床写作既耗时又容易出错。自动化系统将提高标准化、减少错误、缩短时间并提高医疗可及性。在本文中，我们证明了特定领域预训练的重要性，并为医学图像字幕任务提出了一种改进的变压器架构。为了实现这一目标，我们训练了一系列经过修改的变压器，以根据胸片图像输入生成临床报告。这些修改后的变压器包括：带有使用 ImageNet 预训练权重的视觉提取器的网格内存增强变压器架构、带有使用 CheXpert 预训练权重的视觉提取器的网格内存增强变压器架构，以及一个网格内存增强变压器，其编码器使用 ImageNet 预训练权重和 CheXpert 预训练权重传递级联嵌入。我们使用 BLEU(1-4)、ROUGE-L、CIDEr 和临床 CheXbert F1 分数来验证我们的模型，并展示与最先进模型的竞争分数。我们提供的证据表明 ImageNet 预训练不适合医学图像字幕任务，特别是对于不太常见的情况（例如：扩大的心纵隔、肺部病变、气胸）。此外，我们证明双特征模型可以提高特定医疗状况（水肿、实变、气胸、支持设备）和整体 CheXbert F1 评分的性能，并且应该在未来的工作中进一步开发。这种双特征模型，包括 ImageNet 预训练和特定领域预训练，可广泛用于医学中的图像字幕模型。 本文研究使用改进的Transformer架构进行胸部X光临床报告生成，并探讨迁移学习在医学图像字幕任务中的效果。 Edward Vendrow,Ethan Schonfeld eess.IV,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Mayank Golhar,Taylor L. Bobrow,Saowanee Ngamruengphong,Nicholas J. Durr
title: GAN Inversion for Data Augmentation to Improve Colonoscopy Lesion Classification
title_zh: GAN 反演用于数据增强以改进结肠镜检查病变分类
abstract: A major challenge in applying deep learning to medical imaging is the paucity of annotated data. This study demonstrates that synthetic colonoscopy images generated by Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) inversion can be used as training data to improve the lesion classification performance of deep learning models. This approach inverts pairs of images with the same label to a semantically rich & disentangled latent space and manipulates latent representations to produce new synthetic images with the same label. We perform image modality translation (style transfer) between white light and narrowband imaging (NBI). We also generate realistic-looking synthetic lesion images by interpolating between original training images to increase the variety of lesion shapes in the training dataset. We show that these approaches outperform comparative colonoscopy data augmentation techniques without the need to re-train multiple generative models. This approach also leverages information from datasets that may not have been designed for the specific colonoscopy downstream task. E.g. using a bowel prep grading dataset for a polyp classification task. Our experiments show this approach can perform multiple colonoscopy data augmentations, which improve the downstream polyp classification performance over baseline and comparison methods by up to 6%.
summary: A major challenge in applying deep learning to medical imaging is the paucity of annotated data. This study demonstrates that synthetic colonoscopy images generated by Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) inversion can be used as training data to improve the lesion classification performance of deep learning models. This approach inverts pairs of images with the same label to a semantically rich & disentangled latent space and manipulates latent representations to produce new synthetic images with the same label. We perform image modality translation (style transfer) between white light and narrowband imaging (NBI). We also generate realistic-looking synthetic lesion images by interpolating between original training images to increase the variety of lesion shapes in the training dataset. We show that these approaches outperform comparative colonoscopy data augmentation techniques without the need to re-train multiple generative models. This approach also leverages information from datasets that may not have been designed for the specific colonoscopy downstream task. E.g. using a bowel prep grading dataset for a polyp classification task. Our experiments show this approach can perform multiple colonoscopy data augmentations, which improve the downstream polyp classification performance over baseline and comparison methods by up to 6%.
abstract_zh: 将深度学习应用于医学成像的一个主要挑战是注释数据的缺乏。本研究表明，生成对抗网络（GAN）反演生成的合成结肠镜图像可以用作训练数据，以提高深度学习模型的病变分类性能。这种方法将具有相同标签的图像对反转为语义丰富且解缠结的潜在空间，并操纵潜在表示以生成具有相同标签的新合成图像。我们在白光和窄带成像 (NBI) 之间进行图像模态转换（风格转换）。我们还通过在原始训练图像之间进行插值来生成逼真的合成病变图像，以增加训练数据集中病变形状的多样性。我们表明，这些方法优于比较结肠镜检查数据增强技术，而无需重新训练多个生成模型。这种方法还利用了可能不是为特定结肠镜检查下游任务设计的数据集的信息。例如。使用肠道准备分级数据集进行息肉分类任务。我们的实验表明，这种方法可以执行多次结肠镜检查数据增强，与基线和比较方法相比，下游息肉分类性能提高高达 6%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用生成对抗网络（GAN）反转生成合成结肠镜图像，以增强数据并提高息肉分类性能。
AI.motivation: 医学影像中标注数据不足限制了深度学习应用，传统数据增强方法效果有限，需探索更高效的数据生成策略。
AI.method: 通过将相同标签的图像对映射到语义丰富的潜在空间，操控潜在表示生成新图像；进行白光与窄带成像（NBI）的模态转换，并通过插值增加病变形状多样性。
AI.result: 该方法在无需重新训练多个生成模型的情况下，优于对比数据增强技术，使息肉分类性能提升最高达6%。
AI.conclusion: GAN反转方法能有效生成多样化合成数据，提升分类效果，且可跨数据集复用信息，具有临床应用潜力。

authors_text: Dave Epstein,Taesung Park,Richard Zhang,Eli Shechtman,Alexei A. Efros
title: BlobGAN: Spatially Disentangled Scene Representations
title_zh: BlobGAN：空间分离的场景表示
comment: ECCV 2022. Project webpage available at https://www.dave.ml/blobgan
comments: ECCV 2022. Project webpage available at https://www.dave.ml/blobgan
abstract: We propose an unsupervised, mid-level representation for a generative model of scenes. The representation is mid-level in that it is neither per-pixel nor per-image; rather, scenes are modeled as a collection of spatial, depth-ordered "blobs" of features. Blobs are differentiably placed onto a feature grid that is decoded into an image by a generative adversarial network. Due to the spatial uniformity of blobs and the locality inherent to convolution, our network learns to associate different blobs with different entities in a scene and to arrange these blobs to capture scene layout. We demonstrate this emergent behavior by showing that, despite training without any supervision, our method enables applications such as easy manipulation of objects within a scene (e.g., moving, removing, and restyling furniture), creation of feasible scenes given constraints (e.g., plausible rooms with drawers at a particular location), and parsing of real-world images into constituent parts. On a challenging multi-category dataset of indoor scenes, BlobGAN outperforms StyleGAN2 in image quality as measured by FID. See our project page for video results and interactive demo: https://www.dave.ml/blobgan
summary: We propose an unsupervised, mid-level representation for a generative model of scenes. The representation is mid-level in that it is neither per-pixel nor per-image; rather, scenes are modeled as a collection of spatial, depth-ordered "blobs" of features. Blobs are differentiably placed onto a feature grid that is decoded into an image by a generative adversarial network. Due to the spatial uniformity of blobs and the locality inherent to convolution, our network learns to associate different blobs with different entities in a scene and to arrange these blobs to capture scene layout. We demonstrate this emergent behavior by showing that, despite training without any supervision, our method enables applications such as easy manipulation of objects within a scene (e.g., moving, removing, and restyling furniture), creation of feasible scenes given constraints (e.g., plausible rooms with drawers at a particular location), and parsing of real-world images into constituent parts. On a challenging multi-category dataset of indoor scenes, BlobGAN outperforms StyleGAN2 in image quality as measured by FID. See our project page for video results and interactive demo: https://www.dave.ml/blobgan
abstract_zh: 我们为场景的生成模型提出了一种无监督的中级表示。该表示是中等水平的，因为它既不是每个像素也不是每个图像；相反，场景被建模为空间、深度有序的“斑点”特征的集合。 Blob 以可区分的方式放置在特征网格上，该特征网格由生成对抗网络解码为图像。由于斑点的空间均匀性和卷积固有的局部性，我们的网络学习将不同的斑点与场景中的不同实体相关联，并排列这些斑点以捕获场景布局。我们展示了这种突发行为，表明尽管在没有任何监督的情况下进行训练，我们的方法仍然可以实现诸如轻松操纵场景中的对象（例如，移动、移除和重新设计家具）、在给定约束下创建可行的场景（例如，在特定位置有抽屉的合理房间）以及将现实世界图像解析为组成部分等应用。在具有挑战性的室内场景多类别数据集上，通过 FID 测量，BlobGAN 在图像质量方面优于 StyleGAN2。请参阅我们的项目页面以获取视频结果和交互式演示：https://www.dave.ml/blobgan

AI.tldr: 本文提出BlobGAN，一种用于场景生成的中间层次表示方法，通过空间解耦的特征块（blobs）实现场景布局建模。
AI.motivation: 现有生成模型难以同时捕捉场景的全局结构与局部细节，传统像素级或图像级表示无法有效支持场景元素的独立操控与布局调整。
AI.method: 将场景分解为具有深度顺序的特征块（blobs），通过可微分的空间排列在特征网格上，结合GAN进行图像生成，利用卷积局部性实现块与场景实体的关联学习。
AI.result: 在室内场景数据集上以FID指标超越StyleGAN2，支持无监督下的物体移动、删除、重绘及约束场景生成，并可解析真实图像为组成部件。
AI.conclusion: BlobGAN通过空间解耦表示有效提升了场景生成质量与可操控性，为复杂场景的生成与编辑提供了新思路。

ai_content_json.result: 在室内场景数据集上以FID指标超越StyleGAN2，支持无监督下的物体移动、删除、重绘及约束场景生成，并可解析真实图像为组成部件。
ai_content_json.conclusion: BlobGAN通过空间解耦表示有效提升了场景生成质量与可操控性，为复杂场景的生成与编辑提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出BlobGAN，一种用于场景生成的中间层次表示方法，通过空间解耦的特征块（blobs）实现场景布局建模。
motivation: 现有生成模型难以同时捕捉场景的全局结构与局部细节，传统像素级或图像级表示无法有效支持场景元素的独立操控与布局调整。
method: 将场景分解为具有深度顺序的特征块（blobs），通过可微分的空间排列在特征网格上，结合GAN进行图像生成，利用卷积局部性实现块与场景实体的关联学习。
result: 在室内场景数据集上以FID指标超越StyleGAN2，支持无监督下的物体移动、删除、重绘及约束场景生成，并可解析真实图像为组成部件。
conclusion: BlobGAN通过空间解耦表示有效提升了场景生成质量与可操控性，为复杂场景的生成与编辑提供了新思路。
search_text: BlobGAN: Spatially Disentangled Scene Representations BlobGAN：空间分离的场景表示 We propose an unsupervised, mid-level representation for a generative model of scenes. The representation is mid-level in that it is neither per-pixel nor per-image; rather, scenes are modeled as a collection of spatial, depth-ordered "blobs" of features. Blobs are differentiably placed onto a feature grid that is decoded into an image by a generative adversarial network. Due to the spatial uniformity of blobs and the locality inherent to convolution, our network learns to associate different blobs with different entities in a scene and to arrange these blobs to capture scene layout. We demonstrate this emergent behavior by showing that, despite training without any supervision, our method enables applications such as easy manipulation of objects within a scene (e.g., moving, removing, and restyling furniture), creation of feasible scenes given constraints (e.g., plausible rooms with drawers at a particular location), and parsing of real-world images into constituent parts. On a challenging multi-category dataset of indoor scenes, BlobGAN outperforms StyleGAN2 in image quality as measured by FID. See our project page for video results and interactive demo: https://www.dave.ml/blobgan 我们为场景的生成模型提出了一种无监督的中级表示。该表示是中等水平的，因为它既不是每个像素也不是每个图像；相反，场景被建模为空间、深度有序的“斑点”特征的集合。 Blob 以可区分的方式放置在特征网格上，该特征网格由生成对抗网络解码为图像。由于斑点的空间均匀性和卷积固有的局部性，我们的网络学习将不同的斑点与场景中的不同实体相关联，并排列这些斑点以捕获场景布局。我们展示了这种突发行为，表明尽管在没有任何监督的情况下进行训练，我们的方法仍然可以实现诸如轻松操纵场景中的对象（例如，移动、移除和重新设计家具）、在给定约束下创建可行的场景（例如，在特定位置有抽屉的合理房间）以及将现实世界图像解析为组成部分等应用。在具有挑战性的室内场景多类别数据集上，通过 FID 测量，BlobGAN 在图像质量方面优于 StyleGAN2。请参阅我们的项目页面以获取视频结果和交互式演示：https://www.dave.ml/blobgan 本文提出BlobGAN，一种用于场景生成的中间层次表示方法，通过空间解耦的特征块（blobs）实现场景布局建模。 Dave Epstein,Taesung Park,Richard Zhang,Eli Shechtman,Alexei A. Efros cs.CV

authors_text: Haoyu Guo,Sida Peng,Haotong Lin,Qianqian Wang,Guofeng Zhang,Hujun Bao,Xiaowei Zhou
title: Neural 3D Scene Reconstruction with the Manhattan-world Assumption
title_zh: 曼哈顿世界假设下的神经 3D 场景重建
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of reconstructing 3D indoor scenes from multi-view images. Many previous works have shown impressive reconstruction results on textured objects, but they still have difficulty in handling low-textured planar regions, which are common in indoor scenes. An approach to solving this issue is to incorporate planer constraints into the depth map estimation in multi-view stereo-based methods, but the per-view plane estimation and depth optimization lack both efficiency and multi-view consistency. In this work, we show that the planar constraints can be conveniently integrated into the recent implicit neural representation-based reconstruction methods. Specifically, we use an MLP network to represent the signed distance function as the scene geometry. Based on the Manhattan-world assumption, planar constraints are employed to regularize the geometry in floor and wall regions predicted by a 2D semantic segmentation network. To resolve the inaccurate segmentation, we encode the semantics of 3D points with another MLP and design a novel loss that jointly optimizes the scene geometry and semantics in 3D space. Experiments on ScanNet and 7-Scenes datasets show that the proposed method outperforms previous methods by a large margin on 3D reconstruction quality. The code is available at https://zju3dv.github.io/manhattan_sdf.
summary: This paper addresses the challenge of reconstructing 3D indoor scenes from multi-view images. Many previous works have shown impressive reconstruction results on textured objects, but they still have difficulty in handling low-textured planar regions, which are common in indoor scenes. An approach to solving this issue is to incorporate planer constraints into the depth map estimation in multi-view stereo-based methods, but the per-view plane estimation and depth optimization lack both efficiency and multi-view consistency. In this work, we show that the planar constraints can be conveniently integrated into the recent implicit neural representation-based reconstruction methods. Specifically, we use an MLP network to represent the signed distance function as the scene geometry. Based on the Manhattan-world assumption, planar constraints are employed to regularize the geometry in floor and wall regions predicted by a 2D semantic segmentation network. To resolve the inaccurate segmentation, we encode the semantics of 3D points with another MLP and design a novel loss that jointly optimizes the scene geometry and semantics in 3D space. Experiments on ScanNet and 7-Scenes datasets show that the proposed method outperforms previous methods by a large margin on 3D reconstruction quality. The code is available at https://zju3dv.github.io/manhattan_sdf.
abstract_zh: 本文解决了从多视图图像重建 3D 室内场景的挑战。以前的许多工作在纹理对象上显示了令人印象深刻的重建结果，但它们在处理室内场景中常见的低纹理平面区域方面仍然存在困难。解决这个问题的一种方法是将平面约束纳入基于多视图立体方法的深度图估计中，但是每个视图的平面估计和深度优化缺乏效率和多视图一致性。在这项工作中，我们表明平面约束可以方便地集成到最近基于隐式神经表示的重建方法中。具体来说，我们使用 MLP 网络将带符号的距离函数表示为场景几何形状。基于曼哈顿世界假设，采用平面约束来规范二维语义分割网络预测的地板和墙壁区域的几何形状。为了解决分割不准确的问题，我们使用另一个 MLP 对 3D 点的语义进行编码，并设计了一种新颖的损失来联合优化 3D 空间中的场景几何和语义。在 ScanNet 和 7-Scenes 数据集上的实验表明，所提出的方法在 3D 重建质量上大大优于以前的方法。代码可在 https://zju3dv.github.io/manhattan_sdf 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于曼哈顿世界假设的神经3D场景重建方法，旨在解决室内场景中低纹理平面区域重建困难的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统多视角立体视觉方法在处理室内低纹理平面区域时效率低下且缺乏多视角一致性，而基于隐式神经表示的方法尚未有效整合平面约束。
AI.method: 使用MLP网络表示场景的符号距离函数，结合2D语义分割网络的平面约束，并设计联合优化场景几何与语义的三维损失函数。
AI.result: 在ScanNet和7-Scenes数据集上显著优于现有方法，重建质量提升明显。
AI.conclusion: 通过将平面约束与神经表示结合，有效提升了室内场景重建的精度与鲁棒性。

authors_text: Sizhe Li,Zhiao Huang,Tao Du,Hao Su,Joshua B. Tenenbaum,Chuang Gan
title: Contact Points Discovery for Soft-Body Manipulations with Differentiable Physics
title_zh: 利用可微物理发现软体操纵的接触点
abstract: Differentiable physics has recently been shown as a powerful tool for solving soft-body manipulation tasks. However, the differentiable physics solver often gets stuck when the initial contact points of the end effectors are sub-optimal or when performing multi-stage tasks that require contact point switching, which often leads to local minima. To address this challenge, we propose a contact point discovery approach (CPDeform) that guides the stand-alone differentiable physics solver to deform various soft-body plasticines. The key idea of our approach is to integrate optimal transport-based contact points discovery into the differentiable physics solver to overcome the local minima from initial contact points or contact switching. On single-stage tasks, our method can automatically find suitable initial contact points based on transport priorities. On complex multi-stage tasks, we can iteratively switch the contact points of end-effectors based on transport priorities. To evaluate the effectiveness of our method, we introduce PlasticineLab-M that extends the existing differentiable physics benchmark PlasticineLab to seven new challenging multi-stage soft-body manipulation tasks. Extensive experimental results suggest that: 1) on multi-stage tasks that are infeasible for the vanilla differentiable physics solver, our approach discovers contact points that efficiently guide the solver to completion; 2) on tasks where the vanilla solver performs sub-optimally or near-optimally, our contact point discovery method performs better than or on par with the manipulation performance obtained with handcrafted contact points.
summary: Differentiable physics has recently been shown as a powerful tool for solving soft-body manipulation tasks. However, the differentiable physics solver often gets stuck when the initial contact points of the end effectors are sub-optimal or when performing multi-stage tasks that require contact point switching, which often leads to local minima. To address this challenge, we propose a contact point discovery approach (CPDeform) that guides the stand-alone differentiable physics solver to deform various soft-body plasticines. The key idea of our approach is to integrate optimal transport-based contact points discovery into the differentiable physics solver to overcome the local minima from initial contact points or contact switching. On single-stage tasks, our method can automatically find suitable initial contact points based on transport priorities. On complex multi-stage tasks, we can iteratively switch the contact points of end-effectors based on transport priorities. To evaluate the effectiveness of our method, we introduce PlasticineLab-M that extends the existing differentiable physics benchmark PlasticineLab to seven new challenging multi-stage soft-body manipulation tasks. Extensive experimental results suggest that: 1) on multi-stage tasks that are infeasible for the vanilla differentiable physics solver, our approach discovers contact points that efficiently guide the solver to completion; 2) on tasks where the vanilla solver performs sub-optimally or near-optimally, our contact point discovery method performs better than or on par with the manipulation performance obtained with handcrafted contact points.
abstract_zh: 最近，可微物理被证明是解决软体操纵任务的强大工具。然而，当末端执行器的初始接触点不是最优的或者执行需要接触点切换的多阶段任务时，可微物理求解器经常会陷入困境，这通常会导致局部最小值。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种接触点发现方法（CPDeform），该方法指导独立的可微分物理解算器使各种软体橡皮泥变形。我们方法的关键思想是将基于传输的最佳接触点发现集成到可微物理解算器中，以克服初始接触点或接触切换的局部最小值。在单阶段任务中，我们的方法可以根据传输优先级自动找到合适的初始接触点。在复杂的多阶段任务中，我们可以根据传输优先级迭代切换末端执行器的接触点。为了评估我们方法的有效性，我们引入了 PlasticineLab-M，它将现有的可微分物理基准 PlasticineLab 扩展到七个新的具有挑战性的多阶段软体操纵任务。大量的实验结果表明：1）对于普通可微物理求解器无法完成的多阶段任务，我们的方法发现了有效引导求解器完成的接触点； 2）对于普通求解器执行次优或接近最优的任务，我们的接触点发现方法的性能优于或与手工制作的接触点获得的操纵性能相当。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于可微分物理的软体操控接触点发现方法CPDeform，以解决初始接触点不佳或多阶段任务中接触点切换导致的局部最优问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的可微分物理求解器在初始接触点次优或多阶段任务需要接触点切换时容易陷入局部最优，导致软体操控任务失败。
AI.method: 通过将基于最优传输的接触点发现机制集成到可微分物理求解器中，自动寻找单阶段任务的初始接触点，并在多阶段任务中迭代切换接触点。
AI.result: 在扩展的PlasticineLab-M基准测试中，该方法成功解决了原始求解器无法处理的多阶段任务，并在多数任务上优于或匹配合成接触点的手动设计方法。
AI.conclusion: CPDeform通过引入最优传输理论有效克服了软体操控中接触点发现的挑战，提升了可微分物理求解器的鲁棒性和任务完成能力。

search_text: Contact Points Discovery for Soft-Body Manipulations with Differentiable Physics 利用可微物理发现软体操纵的接触点 Differentiable physics has recently been shown as a powerful tool for solving soft-body manipulation tasks. However, the differentiable physics solver often gets stuck when the initial contact points of the end effectors are sub-optimal or when performing multi-stage tasks that require contact point switching, which often leads to local minima. To address this challenge, we propose a contact point discovery approach (CPDeform) that guides the stand-alone differentiable physics solver to deform various soft-body plasticines. The key idea of our approach is to integrate optimal transport-based contact points discovery into the differentiable physics solver to overcome the local minima from initial contact points or contact switching. On single-stage tasks, our method can automatically find suitable initial contact points based on transport priorities. On complex multi-stage tasks, we can iteratively switch the contact points of end-effectors based on transport priorities. To evaluate the effectiveness of our method, we introduce PlasticineLab-M that extends the existing differentiable physics benchmark PlasticineLab to seven new challenging multi-stage soft-body manipulation tasks. Extensive experimental results suggest that: 1) on multi-stage tasks that are infeasible for the vanilla differentiable physics solver, our approach discovers contact points that efficiently guide the solver to completion; 2) on tasks where the vanilla solver performs sub-optimally or near-optimally, our contact point discovery method performs better than or on par with the manipulation performance obtained with handcrafted contact points. 最近，可微物理被证明是解决软体操纵任务的强大工具。然而，当末端执行器的初始接触点不是最优的或者执行需要接触点切换的多阶段任务时，可微物理求解器经常会陷入困境，这通常会导致局部最小值。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种接触点发现方法（CPDeform），该方法指导独立的可微分物理解算器使各种软体橡皮泥变形。我们方法的关键思想是将基于传输的最佳接触点发现集成到可微物理解算器中，以克服初始接触点或接触切换的局部最小值。在单阶段任务中，我们的方法可以根据传输优先级自动找到合适的初始接触点。在复杂的多阶段任务中，我们可以根据传输优先级迭代切换末端执行器的接触点。为了评估我们方法的有效性，我们引入了 PlasticineLab-M，它将现有的可微分物理基准 PlasticineLab 扩展到七个新的具有挑战性的多阶段软体操纵任务。大量的实验结果表明：1）对于普通可微物理求解器无法完成的多阶段任务，我们的方法发现了有效引导求解器完成的接触点； 2）对于普通求解器执行次优或接近最优的任务，我们的接触点发现方法的性能优于或与手工制作的接触点获得的操纵性能相当。 本文提出了一种基于可微分物理的软体操控接触点发现方法CPDeform，以解决初始接触点不佳或多阶段任务中接触点切换导致的局部最优问题。 Sizhe Li,Zhiao Huang,Tao Du,Hao Su,Joshua B. Tenenbaum,Chuang Gan cs.RO,cs.CV,cs.GR,cs.LG

authors_text: Yining Hong,Kaichun Mo,Li Yi,Leonidas J. Guibas,Antonio Torralba,Joshua B. Tenenbaum,Chuang Gan
title: Fixing Malfunctional Objects With Learned Physical Simulation and Functional Prediction
title_zh: 通过学习的物理模拟和功能预测修复故障对象
abstract: This paper studies the problem of fixing malfunctional 3D objects. While previous works focus on building passive perception models to learn the functionality from static 3D objects, we argue that functionality is reckoned with respect to the physical interactions between the object and the user. Given a malfunctional object, humans can perform mental simulations to reason about its functionality and figure out how to fix it. Inspired by this, we propose FixIt, a dataset that contains about 5k poorly-designed 3D physical objects paired with choices to fix them. To mimic humans' mental simulation process, we present FixNet, a novel framework that seamlessly incorporates perception and physical dynamics. Specifically, FixNet consists of a perception module to extract the structured representation from the 3D point cloud, a physical dynamics prediction module to simulate the results of interactions on 3D objects, and a functionality prediction module to evaluate the functionality and choose the correct fix. Experimental results show that our framework outperforms baseline models by a large margin, and can generalize well to objects with similar interaction types.
summary: This paper studies the problem of fixing malfunctional 3D objects. While previous works focus on building passive perception models to learn the functionality from static 3D objects, we argue that functionality is reckoned with respect to the physical interactions between the object and the user. Given a malfunctional object, humans can perform mental simulations to reason about its functionality and figure out how to fix it. Inspired by this, we propose FixIt, a dataset that contains about 5k poorly-designed 3D physical objects paired with choices to fix them. To mimic humans' mental simulation process, we present FixNet, a novel framework that seamlessly incorporates perception and physical dynamics. Specifically, FixNet consists of a perception module to extract the structured representation from the 3D point cloud, a physical dynamics prediction module to simulate the results of interactions on 3D objects, and a functionality prediction module to evaluate the functionality and choose the correct fix. Experimental results show that our framework outperforms baseline models by a large margin, and can generalize well to objects with similar interaction types.
abstract_zh: 本文研究修复故障 3D 对象的问题。虽然之前的工作重点是构建被动感知模型以从静态 3D 对象中学习功能，但我们认为功能是根据对象与用户之间的物理交互来计算的。给定一个出现故障的物体，人类可以进行心理模拟来推理其功能并找出修复方法。受此启发，我们提出了 FixIt，这是一个数据集，其中包含大约 5k 个设计不良的 3D 物理对象以及修复它们的选项。为了模仿人类的心理模拟过程，我们提出了 FixNet，这是一种无缝融合感知和物理动力学的新颖框架。具体来说，FixNet 由一个用于从 3D 点云中提取结构化表示的感知模块、一个用于模拟 3D 对象交互结果的物理动力学预测模块以及一个用于评估功能并选择正确修复的功能预测模块组成。实验结果表明，我们的框架大大优于基线模型，并且可以很好地推广到具有相似交互类型的对象。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合物理模拟与功能预测的框架，用于修复有缺陷的3D物体。
AI.motivation: 传统方法仅从静态3D物体学习功能，而实际功能需通过物体与用户的物理互动来判断，需模拟人类心智过程。
AI.method: 构建FixIt数据集并设计FixNet框架，包含感知模块提取3D结构、物理模拟模块预测交互结果、功能预测模块选择修复方案。
AI.result: 框架在基准模型上显著提升性能，且能有效推广至类似交互类型的物体。
AI.conclusion: 通过物理模拟与功能预测结合，可准确识别并修复有缺陷物体的交互功能。

authors_text: Brady Zhou,Philipp Krähenbühl
title: Cross-view Transformers for real-time Map-view Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 用于实时地图视图语义分割的跨视图变压器
abstract: We present cross-view transformers, an efficient attention-based model for map-view semantic segmentation from multiple cameras. Our architecture implicitly learns a mapping from individual camera views into a canonical map-view representation using a camera-aware cross-view attention mechanism. Each camera uses positional embeddings that depend on its intrinsic and extrinsic calibration. These embeddings allow a transformer to learn the mapping across different views without ever explicitly modeling it geometrically. The architecture consists of a convolutional image encoder for each view and cross-view transformer layers to infer a map-view semantic segmentation. Our model is simple, easily parallelizable, and runs in real-time. The presented architecture performs at state-of-the-art on the nuScenes dataset, with 4x faster inference speeds. Code is available at https://github.com/bradyz/cross_view_transformers.
summary: We present cross-view transformers, an efficient attention-based model for map-view semantic segmentation from multiple cameras. Our architecture implicitly learns a mapping from individual camera views into a canonical map-view representation using a camera-aware cross-view attention mechanism. Each camera uses positional embeddings that depend on its intrinsic and extrinsic calibration. These embeddings allow a transformer to learn the mapping across different views without ever explicitly modeling it geometrically. The architecture consists of a convolutional image encoder for each view and cross-view transformer layers to infer a map-view semantic segmentation. Our model is simple, easily parallelizable, and runs in real-time. The presented architecture performs at state-of-the-art on the nuScenes dataset, with 4x faster inference speeds. Code is available at https://github.com/bradyz/cross_view_transformers.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了跨视图转换器，这是一种基于注意力的高效模型，用于来自多个摄像机的地图视图语义分割。我们的架构使用相机感知的跨视图注意机制隐式学习从单个相机视图到规范地图视图表示的映射。每个相机都使用取决于其内在和外在校准的位置嵌入。这些嵌入允许转换器学习不同视图之间的映射，而无需显式地对其进行几何建模。该架构由每个视图的卷积图像编码器和跨视图变换层组成，以推断地图视图语义分割。我们的模型很简单，易于并行化，并且实时运行。所提出的架构在 nuScenes 数据集上具有最先进的性能，推理速度提高了 4 倍。代码可在 https://github.com/bradyz/cross_view_transformers 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于交叉视图变换器的高效注意力模型，用于从多个摄像头实时生成地图视图语义分割。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖复杂的几何建模进行多视角到地图视图的转换，计算成本高且难以实时处理。本文旨在开发一种无需显式几何建模、能实时运行的简单高效模型。
AI.method: 架构包含每个视角的卷积图像编码器和交叉视图变换器层，通过摄像头感知的位置嵌入（依赖内外参校准）隐式学习视角到地图视图的映射，无需显式几何建模。
AI.result: 在nuScenes数据集上达到SOTA性能，推理速度较现有方法快4倍，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 交叉视图变换器通过隐式学习视角映射，在保持高精度的同时实现了实时地图视图语义分割，验证了其效率与有效性。

authors_text: Vladimir Guzov,Julian Chibane,Riccardo Marin,Yannan He,Yunus Saracoglu,Torsten Sattler,Gerard Pons-Moll
title: Interaction Replica: Tracking Human-Object Interaction and Scene Changes From Human Motion
title_zh: 交互复制品：跟踪人与物体的交互和人体运动的场景变化
abstract: Our world is not static and humans naturally cause changes in their environments through interactions, e.g., opening doors or moving furniture. Modeling changes caused by humans is essential for building digital twins, e.g., in the context of shared physical-virtual spaces (metaverses) and robotics. In order for widespread adoption of such emerging applications, the sensor setup used to capture the interactions needs to be inexpensive and easy-to-use for non-expert users. I.e., interactions should be captured and modeled by simple ego-centric sensors such as a combination of cameras and IMU sensors, not relying on any external cameras or object trackers. Yet, to the best of our knowledge, no work tackling the challenging problem of modeling human-scene interactions via such an ego-centric sensor setup exists. This paper closes this gap in the literature by developing a novel approach that combines visual localization of humans in the scene with contact-based reasoning about human-scene interactions from IMU data. Interestingly, we can show that even without visual observations of the interactions, human-scene contacts and interactions can be realistically predicted from human pose sequences. Our method, iReplica (Interaction Replica), is an essential first step towards the egocentric capture of human interactions and modeling of dynamic scenes, which is required for future AR/VR applications in immersive virtual universes and for training machines to behave like humans. Our code, data and model are available on our project page at http://virtualhumans.mpi-inf.mpg.de/ireplica/

This paper closes this gap in the literature by developing a novel approach that combines visual localization of humans in the scene with contact-based reasoning about human-scene interactions from IMU data. Interestingly, we can show that even without visual observations of the interactions, human-scene contacts and interactions can be realistically predicted from human pose sequences. Our method, iReplica (Interaction Replica), is an essential first step towards the egocentric capture of human interactions and modeling of dynamic scenes, which is required for future AR/VR applications in immersive virtual universes and for training machines to behave like humans. Our code, data and model are available on our project page at http://virtualhumans.mpi-inf.mpg.de/ireplica/
abstract_zh: 我们的世界不是静态的，人类自然地通过相互作用引起环境的变化，例如打开门或移动家具。对人类引起的变化进行建模对于构建数字孪生至关重要，例如在共享物理虚拟空间（元空间）和机器人技术的背景下。为了广泛采用此类新兴应用，用于捕获交互的传感器设置必须便宜且易于非专家用户使用。也就是说，交互应该通过简单的以自我为中心的传感器（例如相机和 IMU 传感器的组合）来捕获和建模，而不依赖于任何外部相机或对象跟踪器。然而，据我们所知，目前还没有任何工作可以解决通过这种以自我为中心的传感器设置来建模人类场景交互这一具有挑战性的问题。本文通过开发一种新颖的方法来弥补文献中的这一空白，该方法将场景中人类的视觉定位与基于 IMU 数据的人与场景交互的基于接触的推理相结合。有趣的是，我们可以证明，即使没有对交互的视觉观察，也可以从人类姿势序列中真实地预测人类场景的接触和交互。我们的方法 iReplica（交互复制）是朝着以自我为中心捕获人类交互和动态场景建模迈出的重要的第一步，这是未来沉浸式虚拟宇宙中的 AR/VR 应用以及训练机器像人类一样行为所必需的。我们的代码、数据和模型可在我们的项目页面上找到：http://virtual humans.mpi-inf.mpg.de/ireplica/

AI.tldr: 本文提出iReplica方法，通过第一人称视角的传感器（如摄像头和IMU）追踪人类与物体的互动及场景变化，无需外部设备或视觉观察。
AI.motivation: 现有技术依赖昂贵或复杂的外部设备来捕捉人类与环境的交互，缺乏低成本、易用的第一人称传感器方案，限制了元宇宙和机器人等应用的发展。
AI.method: 结合场景中的人体视觉定位与IMU数据的接触推理，仅通过人体姿态序列预测人-场景互动及环境变化，无需直接视觉观察交互过程。
AI.result: 成功实现对人-物交互及动态场景的建模，为沉浸式虚拟应用和机器学习人类行为提供基础，代码与数据已开源。
AI.conclusion: iReplica填补了第一人称交互建模的空白，是构建数字孪生体和推进AR/VR应用的关键技术突破。
